contents:
(** * Coreflective subuniverses. *)

(** In this file we study "coreflective subuniverses" that are defined dually to reflective subuniverses.  However, it turns out that there are many fewer examples of these.  The "internal" nature of such definitions, which in the reflective case makes the subuniverse automatically an exponential ideal, in the coreflective case has much stronger consequences: it forces the entire coreflection to be determined by the image of [Unit], which can be an arbitrary hprop.  Thus, this file is essentially just a no-go theorem: there are no coreflective subuniverses other than a certain class of fairly simple ones (which we call "co-open" since they are dual to open modalities).

In particular, since we do not foresee many applications of this file, we don't bother introducing modules to make the definitions more universe polymorphic the way we did for reflective subuniverses. *)
(** We begin by extracting the corecursor, its computation rule, and its eta principle. *)
Definition F_corec {Y X} `(inF F Y) (f : Y -> X) : Y -> F X.

goals.conclusion: Y -> F X
Y -> F X

contents: refine ((fun (g : Y -> F X) => fromF F X o g)^-1 f).

goals.conclusion: IsEquiv (fun (g : Y -> F X) (x : Y) => fromF F X (g x))

contents:
Definition F_corec_beta {Y X} (YF : inF F Y) (f : Y -> X)
  : fromF F X o F_corec YF f == f.

goals.conclusion: fromF F X o F_corec YF f == f
fromF F X o F_corec YF f == f

contents: apply ap10, (eisretr (fun g => fromF F X o g)).

Definition F_coindpaths {Y X} `(inF F Y) (g h : Y -> F X)
             (p : fromF F X o g == fromF F X o h)
  : g == h.

goals.conclusion: g == h

hypotheses.type: fromF F X o g == fromF F X o h

goals.conclusion: g == h

hypotheses.type: fromF F X o g == fromF F X o h

goals.conclusion: g = h

hypotheses.type: fromF F X o g == fromF F X o h

contents: refine (equiv_inj (fun k => fromF F X o k) _).

goals.conclusion: IsEquiv (fun (k : Y -> F X) (x : Y) => fromF F X (k x))

hypotheses.type: fromF F X o g == fromF F X o h

goals.conclusion: (fun x : Y => fromF F X (g x)) = (fun x : Y => fromF F X (h x))

hypotheses.type: fromF F X o g == fromF F X o h

goals.conclusion: IsEquiv (fun (k : Y -> F X) (x : Y) => fromF F X (k x))

hypotheses.type: fromF F X o g == fromF F X o h

goals.conclusion: (fun x : Y => fromF F X (g x)) = (fun x : Y => fromF F X (h x))

hypotheses.type: fromF F X o g == fromF F X o h

contents: (** The functorial action of the coreflector. *)

Definition F_functor {X Y} (f : X -> Y) : F X -> F Y
    := F_corec (F_inF F X) (f o fromF F X).
(** The coreflector preserves hprops (since it is a right adjoint and thus preserves limits). *)
Local Instance ishprop_coreflection A `{IsHProp A} : IsHProp (F A).

goals.conclusion: IsHProp (F A)
IsHProp (F A)
x = y

contents:
exact (F_coindpaths (F_inF F A) (const x) (const y)
                        (fun _ => path_ishprop _ _) x).
(** A type lies in [F] as soon as [fromF] admits a section. *)

Definition inF_fromF_sect X (s : X -> F X) (p : fromF F X o s == idmap)
  : inF F X.

goals.conclusion: inF F X

hypotheses.type: fromF F X o s == idmap

goals.conclusion: inF F X

hypotheses.type: fromF F X o s == idmap

contents: refine (transport (inF F) (path_universe (fromF F X)) (F_inF F X)).

goals.conclusion: IsEquiv (fromF F X)

hypotheses.type: fromF F X o s == idmap

goals.conclusion: (fun x : F X => s (fromF F X x)) == idmap

hypotheses.type: fromF F X o s == idmap

contents: change (s o fromF F X == idmap).

goals.conclusion: s o fromF F X == idmap

hypotheses.type: fromF F X o s == idmap

goals.conclusion: (fun x : F X => fromF F X (s (fromF F X x))) == (fun x : F X => fromF F X x)

hypotheses.type: fromF F X o s == idmap

contents: (** So far, nothing unexpected has happened.  Now, however, we claim that [F] is completely determined by the image of [Unit], which by [ishprop_coreflection] is an hprop.  Specifically, we claim that [X] lies in [F] exactly when [X -> F Unit]. *)

Definition inF_equiv_implies_funit X
  : inF F X <~> (X -> F Unit).

goals.conclusion: inF F X <~> (X -> F Unit)
inF F X <~> (X -> F Unit)
inF F X -> X -> F Unit
(X -> F Unit) -> inF F X
inF F X -> X -> F Unit
X -> F Unit
X -> Unit
(X -> F Unit) -> inF F X
inF F X
X -> F X
fromF F X o ?s == idmap
X -> F X
F X
fromF F X o (fun x : X => F_functor (unit_name x) (f x)) == idmap
fromF F X (F_corec (F_inF F Unit) (fun _ : F Unit => x) (f x)) = x

contents: (** Conversely, we will now show that for any hprop [U], the types [X] such that [X -> U] are a coreflective subuniverse, which we call "co-open" since it is dual to the open modality. *)

goals.conclusion: CoreflectiveSubuniverse
CoreflectiveSubuniverse

contents:
simple refine (Build_CoreflectiveSubuniverse
              (fun X => Build_HProp (X -> U))
              (fun X => X * U)
              (fun X => @snd X U)
              (fun X => @fst X U) _); try exact _.

goals.conclusion:
forall Y X : Type,
(fun X0 : Type => Build_HProp (X0 -> U)) Y ->
IsEquiv
  (fun g : Y -> (fun X0 : Type => X0 * U) X => (fun X0 : Type => fst) X o g)

contents: intros Y X YU; simpl in *.

goals.conclusion: IsEquiv (fun (g : Y -> X * U) (x : Y) => fst (g x))

contents: refine (isequiv_adjointify _ (fun h y => (h y , YU y)) _ _).

goals.conclusion: (fun (x : Y -> X) (x0 : Y) => fst (x x0, YU x0)) == idmap
(fun (x : Y -> X * U) (y : Y) => (fst (x y), YU y)) == idmap
(fun (x : Y -> X) (x0 : Y) => fst (x x0, YU x0)) == idmap

contents: intros g; apply path_arrow; intros y; reflexivity.

goals.conclusion: (fun (x : Y -> X * U) (y : Y) => (fst (x y), YU y)) == idmap

contents: intros h; apply path_arrow; intros y.

goals.conclusion: (fst (h y), YU y) = h y

contents: apply path_prod; [ reflexivity | by apply path_ishprop ].

(** Thus, each coreflective subuniverses are uniquely determined by an hprop.  Moreover, the coreflective subuniverse corresponding to an hprop [U] is closely related to the open modality [Op U].  Specifically, they form an _adjoint modality pair_ in the sense that the subuniverses are canonically equivalent, and the coreflection and reflection respect this equivalence.  In categorical language, this says that the inclusion of an open subtopos is the center of a local geometric morphism in the other direction.  We express this concisely as follows. *)

Definition coopen_isequiv_open X
  : IsEquiv (O_functor (Op U) (fromF coOp X)).

goals.conclusion: O_inverts (Op U) (fromF coOp X)
O_inverts (Op U) (fromF coOp X)

contents: refine (isequiv_adjointify _ (fun ux => fun u => (ux u , u)) _ _).

goals.conclusion:
(fun x : U -> X => O_functor (Op U) (fromF coOp X) (fun u : U => (x u, u))) ==
idmap

(fun (x : U -> X * U) (u : U) => (O_functor (Op U) (fromF coOp X) x u, u)) ==
idmap

(fun x : U -> X => O_functor (Op U) (fromF coOp X) (fun u : U => (x u, u))) ==
idmap
O_functor (Op U) fst (fun u : U => (ux u, u)) = ux
O_functor (Op U) fst (fun u0 : U => (ux u0, u0)) u = ux u

contents: transitivity (O_functor (Op U) fst (to (Op U) (X * U) (ux u , u)) u).

goals.conclusion:
O_functor (Op U) fst (fun u0 : U => (ux u0, u0)) u =
O_functor (Op U) fst (to (Op U) (X * U) (ux u, u)) u
O_functor (Op U) fst (to (Op U) (X * U) (ux u, u)) u = ux u

O_functor (Op U) fst (fun u0 : U => (ux u0, u0)) u =
O_functor (Op U) fst (to (Op U) (X * U) (ux u, u)) u

contents: apply ap10, ap, path_arrow; intros u'; simpl in *.

goals.conclusion: (ux u', u') = (ux u, u)

contents: apply path_prod; simpl; [ apply ap | ]; apply path_ishprop.

goals.conclusion: O_functor (Op U) fst (to (Op U) (X * U) (ux u, u)) u = ux u

contents: exact (ap10 (to_O_natural (Op U) (@fst X U) (ux u , u)) u).

goals.conclusion:
(fun (x : U -> X * U) (u : U) => (O_functor (Op U) (fromF coOp X) x u, u)) ==
idmap
(fun u : U => (O_functor (Op U) fst uux u, u)) = uux
(O_functor (Op U) fst uux u, u) = uux u
O_functor (Op U) fst uux u = fst (uux u)

contents: transitivity (O_functor (Op U) fst (to (Op U) _ (fst (uux u) , u)) u).

goals.conclusion:
O_functor (Op U) fst uux u =
O_functor (Op U) fst (to (Op U) (X * U) (fst (uux u), u)) u
O_functor (Op U) fst (to (Op U) (X * U) (fst (uux u), u)) u = fst (uux u)

O_functor (Op U) fst uux u =
O_functor (Op U) fst (to (Op U) (X * U) (fst (uux u), u)) u

contents: apply ap10, ap, path_arrow; intros u'.

goals.conclusion: uux u' = to (Op U) (X * U) (fst (uux u), u) u'
fst (uux u') = fst (uux u)
snd (uux u') = u
fst (uux u') = fst (uux u)
snd (uux u') = u
O_functor (Op U) fst (to (Op U) (X * U) (fst (uux u), u)) u = fst (uux u)

contents: exact (ap10 (to_O_natural (Op U) (@fst X U) (fst (uux u) , u)) u).

Definition coopen_equiv_open X
  : Op U (coOp X) <~> Op U X
    := Build_Equiv _ _ (O_functor (Op U) (fromF coOp X))
                  (coopen_isequiv_open X).
